summary: Single-story rectangular pavilion with a raised stone brick perimeter base, oak plank walls with vertical log corner posts, a dark nether-brick stepped hipped roof capped and accented by stone slab/stonebrick blocks, and a continuous row of glass windows on the visible long elevation. The roof has symmetric stepped terraces and a small central cap feature.

shape.overall_form: rectangular block with inset roof and surrounding stone base
shape.symmetry: mostly symmetric along both long and short axes (roof steps are mirrored)

materials.name: slab_stone
materials.role: roof/edge accents (light grey slabs along roof tiers and corners)

elements: continuous row of 5 small windows on each long side (glass panes)
stonebrick surrounding raised edge platform one block high with an outer stair-like slab layer
vertical log corner posts at least 2 blocks tall
oak plank walls between corner logs
dark brick stepped hipped roof with stone slab accent blocks on each step and central cap
rebuild_hints: Start with a rectangular oak-plank box approx 11x11 internal with 1-block thick walls and vertical logs at four corners.
Place a 1-block-high stonebrick border/pedestal around the house footprint; add a one-block outer ledge of stone slabs as shown.
Install a continuous band of glass windows centered on the wall height between logs (windows are 1-high glass panes separated by planks).

Construct a stepped hip roof using dark nether-brick (or dark brick) rising toward center in even terraces; place light grey stone slabs/stonebrick blocks on the outer edge of each terrace and at corners, plus a small central cap.
Ensure roof overhang aligns with the stone coping band that sits on top of the wall (stone coping encircles the building under the roof).
uncertainties: Exact internal floor plan and presence of doors or interior partitions not visible from provided views.
Precise block types for dark roof (nether_brick vs dark_oak slabs textured) — identified as dark nether-brick with moderate confidence.
Whether stone slab accents are stone_slab or smooth_stone_slab vs stonebrick slabs — identified generically as slab_stone with medium confidence.
Exact exterior dimension counts: estimated 13x13 footprint from aerial images; could be off by +/- 1-2 blocks.
